title: Here’s a quick guide to the my new favourite AI trend: ‘vibe coding’ What is vibe coding? • Vibe coding might seem like a meme but is actually very real and has been popularized by legendary… | Robleh Jama | 15 comments
author: Robleh Jama
text:
Here’s a quick guide to the my new favourite AI trend: ‘vibe coding’ What is vibe coding? • Vibe coding might seem like a meme but is actually very real and has been popularized by legendary programmer Andrej Karpathy • It involves writing text prompts for LLMs like Claude Sonnet 3.5 to build software • it’s wild that unlike traditional programming, you can simply write prompts or speak to an LLM and it spits out code What are the tools necessary for vibe coding? At its core, you just need an LLM you can prompt. But the universe of vibe coding tools includes some of the fastest growing companies with small teams. Cursor hit $100m ARR with under 30 employees while Bolt hit $20m ARR within 8 weeks. Here are the top players and the tools I’ve been testing: • Cursor • Lovable • v0 by Vercel • Bolt • Replit • Windsurf • Tempo Labs All of these tools require you to write prompts. You’re essentially chatting with an AI agent that writes code for you. You can also use a speech to text dictation app like Superwhisper for a hands-free vibe coding experience. How is vibe coding changing the game? • You don’t need a degree in Comp Sci or a background in development to build anymore. Non-technical PMs, designers, founders can build projects by themselves (and for themselves) • For technical founders, the time to develop prototypes is shortened. And AI codegen tools make them way more efficient. • Personal software is easier to build than ever. If you’ve ever felt “I wish I had a simple app that could help with [xyz]” you now have the tools to build it. My experience with vibe coding: • I’ve been having a blast spending nights and weekends vibe coding and testing out all of the new AI codegen tools. • After nearly a decade and a half of working in tech, I'm finally building my own software. A lot of them are just silly toys but it's fun and a great way to learn to code. • My goal is to see if I can make production ready apps (i don’t think the tools are there yet), and stretch goal for this year is to start contributing code to Boom and building small features (not just making copy changes) Whether you’re an experienced programmer or someone who has never written a line of code before, I’d encourage you to try some of these tools and give vibe coding a shot - it’s a great way to experience the magic of AI first hand. I recommend starting with Replit. Is there an app you’ve always wanted to build for yourself but haven’t had the skill/time to do so?
These AI dev tools have certainly come a long way, but currently I'm finding it challenging to build anything beyond very simple tools (in Replit and Lovable so far). I find myself spending a lot time fixing bugs/errors even for a simple auth flow. I think now the critical skills needed will shift to planning and structuring prompts, rather than coding or design. At this pace though, I'm sure we'll get there in no time.
This is my experience: Tools like Co-pilot (including Cursor) are more reliable for building product applications. The rest are better suited for micro-prototyping, meaning small features and components with no sophisticated logic. They offer a quick start and can build a few features and processes quickly; however, they often get stuck on simple tasks or error handling. Additionally, like any junior developer, they may unintentionally break existing features while working on new ones, even when the changes are logically unrelated. So, while they are far from being reliable for production-ready apps, they are great for ideation and prototyping.
I was using Replit over the weekend to create a family productivity app. Loved the experience, ease and how far I got with no $ commitment!!
Prompt 1: linkedin.com/in/narjeesh – Using my profile, please create a simple, modern personal website for me. Prompt 2: Please deploy this website on Firebase. We built and launched a personal portfolio website in just minutes! Using Cursor + Composer + Agent 🚀 With Agent AI, coding and deploying websites has never been easier—anyone can do it, and fast!
I like the idea of opening a ticket, writing a user story & having an app do a quick contextually aware search through internal resources for related artifacts to attach. Not just results that match key words to file names, but actually has full LLM analysis on relevance before suggesting a link.
